action.reasoning: Observation: initial run crashed with exit 139 after ~351s before eval, likely due to memory pressure from loading full train/val .pt into Python lists plus multiworker DataLoader/pin_memory on a very large dataset. Analysis: the code currently torch.loads entire train and val objects and keeps all lists in RAM; with 350k graphs this can exceed memory and segfault. We need a lighter first-pass baseline that still satisfies early-eval requirement. Decision: switch to OGB's built-in PCBA dataset loader (already installed, processed locally), use official split indices, smaller model/batch, safer num_workers=0, and robust predict.py using the same dataset format. Then run 2 epochs and submit eval immediately.
